description: The East Harlem Purple Gang was an Italian-American organized crime group based in New York City, active from the early 1970s to the 1980s. It consisted of hit-men and heroin dealers who were semi-independent from the Italian-American Mafia but allied with several crime families, particularly the Genovese family. The gang dominated heroin distribution in East Harlem, Italian Harlem, and the Bronx. At its peak, it had about 30 members and 80 associates, and was considered an unofficial sixth family.
